title: Euphorbia eleanoriae
intro: Euphorbia eleanoriae is a succulent plant species native to the tropical regions of East Africa, characterized by its unique, succulent leaves and stems that store water, enabling it to thrive in arid environments. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Euphorbia eleanoriae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Euphorbia eleanoriae is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional remedies, such as tonics and ointments.
